functions.name: eq
functions.description: Compare two numbers for equality. Float-aware: if either operand is a float, both are compared as floats; otherwise compared as integers.

functions.name: neq
functions.description: Compare two integers for inequality.

functions.name: gt
functions.description: Test whether the left operand is strictly greater than the right.

functions.name: lt
functions.description: Test whether the left operand is strictly less than the right. Float-aware: if either operand resolves to a float both are compared as floats.

functions.name: gte
functions.description: Test whether the left operand is greater than or equal to the right.

functions.name: lte
functions.description: Test whether the left operand is less than or equal to the right.

functions.name: set
functions.description: Assign a value to one or more variables in the current scope. Supports two calling forms: Full form:     {"set": {"var": "$x", "value": 42}} Shorthand:     {"set": {"x": 42, "y": 10}}  (each key is a variable name)

args.name: var
args.description: Variable name to assign (with or without leading $)

args.name: value
args.description: Value to assign to the variable

functions.name: add
functions.description: Add two numbers and store the result in $_.

functions.name: sub
functions.description: Subtract one number from another and store the result in $_.

functions.name: inc
functions.description: Increment a variable in the current scope by 1. The variable is created with value 1 if it does not already exist. Usage: {"inc": "$varName"}

args.name: var
args.description: Variable name to increment (with or without leading $)

functions.name: dec
functions.description: Decrement a variable in the current scope by 1. The variable is created with value -1 if it does not already exist. Usage: {"dec": "$varName"}

args.name: var
args.description: Variable name to decrement (with or without leading $)

functions.name: strcmp
functions.description: Compare two strings using C strcmp semantics. Stores the comparison result in $_ for use with the 'to' mechanism.

returns.description: 0 if equal, negative if a < b, positive if a > b (stored in $_)

functions.name: strlen
functions.description: Return the byte length of a string and store it in $_.

returns.description: Byte length of the string (stored in $_)

functions.name: charat
functions.description: Extract a single character from a string at the given index and store it in $_ as a one-character string. Out-of-bounds access stores an empty string.

returns.description: Single-character string at position index (stored in $_)

functions.name: getbyte
functions.description: Read a single byte from a byte-array variable at the given index and store the value in $_.  Sets $! to MTA_ERR_UNDEFINED_VAR if the variable is not found, or MTA_ERR_ARRAY_BOUNDS if the index is out of range.

args.name: array
args.description: Name of the byte-array variable (with or without leading $)

returns.description: Byte value 0-255 (stored in $_); $! is set on error

functions.name: setbyte
functions.description: Write a single byte into a byte-array variable at the given index. Sets $! to MTA_ERR_UNDEFINED_VAR if the variable is not found, or MTA_ERR_ARRAY_BOUNDS if the index is out of range.

args.name: array
args.description: Name of the byte-array variable (with or without leading $)

functions.name: mul
functions.description: Multiply two numbers and store the result in $_.  Float-aware: if either operand is a float the result is a float; otherwise an integer.

functions.name: div
functions.description: Divide two numbers and store the result in $_.  Float-aware: if either operand is a float the result is a float; otherwise integer division. On division by zero sets $! to MTA_ERR_DIV_ZERO and stores 0 in $_.

returns.description: Quotient a / b (stored in $_); $! set on division by zero

functions.name: mod
functions.description: Compute the integer remainder of a divided by b and store it in $_. Sets $! to MTA_ERR_DIV_ZERO and stores 0 in $_ when b is zero.

returns.description: Remainder a % b (stored in $_); $! set on division by zero

functions.name: round
functions.description: Round a floating-point number to the nearest integer and store it in $_. If the input is already an integer it is passed through unchanged.

functions.name: chr
functions.description: Convert a Latin-1 code point (0-255) to a single-character string and store it in $_.  Values outside 0-255 are mapped to '?'.

functions.name: to_string
functions.description: Convert a number to its decimal string representation and store it in $_. Integers are formatted without a decimal point; floats use %g notation. If the value is already a string it is passed through unchanged.

functions.name: concat
functions.description: Concatenate two or more values into a single string and store it in $_. Each argument is converted to a string before joining.  The total result is capped at 255 bytes.

functions.name: get_config
functions.description: Read a value from the calling plugin's configuration store and return it in $_.  Returns the default value if the key does not exist, or -1 if no default is provided.  Supports int, bool, and string config types.

args.name: default
args.description: Default value to return when the key is not set

functions.name: set_config
functions.description: Write a value to the calling plugin's configuration store.  The config type is inferred from the argument (string → string, float → int, other → int). If a config-save handler is registered the config is persisted immediately.
